prompt_text: Raycast vs Spotlight pricing and features
response:
Raycast is a far more feature‑rich launcher than Spotlight and uses a freemium model, while Spotlight is more limited but completely free and built into macOS.[1][4]  

### Pricing

**Spotlight (Apple)**  
- **Price:** **Free**; included with macOS on every Mac.[1]  
- **No upgrades or subscriptions** to unlock more features; capabilities only change when macOS is updated.

**Raycast**  
- **Core app:** **Free for personal use** with no time limit and no usage caps for most features.[2][4]  
- **Pro / paid tiers:**  
  - Subscription unlocks **AI features, cloud sync, and themes**.[2][4]  
  - Advanced AI is an additional paid add‑on (separate from the Pro discount for students).[4]  
  - Student discount: **50% off Raycast Pro** for verified students (does *not* apply to Advanced AI add‑on).[4]

### Core feature comparison

| Aspect / Feature | Spotlight | Raycast |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Availability** | **Built into macOS**, enabled by default.[1][4] | **Third‑party app**, must be downloaded and installed.[1] |
| **Price model** | 100% free, no tiers.[1] | Free core app; paid Pro plan for AI/sync/themes, plus optional Advanced AI add‑on.[2][4] |
| **Local search** | Searches apps, documents, files, some system info, and suggests web results.[1][4] | Searches apps and files; many users find it **faster and more accurate** than Spotlight.[1][2] |
| **Calculator & quick tools** | Basic inline math and unit lookup; often opens Calculator app when pressing Enter.[3] | Inline calculator that shows the **result directly** and lets you use it immediately.[1][3] |
| **Beyond search (utilities)** | Basic calculator, dictionary, and web search links only.[1] | Built‑in **clipboard history, text snippets, window management, floating notes, system controls** (lock, restart, empty trash, etc.).[1][2][4] |
| **Integrations** | Limited; mainly Apple services and Siri suggestions.[1][4] | Huge library of **extensions** for Jira, GitHub, Notion, Slack, Figma, VS Code, Google services, Spotify, etc., controllable directly from Raycast.[1][4] |
| **Automation / workflows** | Minimal quick actions.[1] | **Highly scriptable**: custom commands, script commands, advanced workflows, command palette for many apps.[1][4] |
| **Web search** | Shows links to web results via default browser.[1] | Custom **search aliases** (e.g., type “g” then query to search Google directly) and many online service integrations.[1][3] |
| **AI features** | Limited to system‑level things like Live Text and Siri; no integrated generative AI in Spotlight itself.[1] | Pro plan unlocks **generative AI**: instant answers, summaries, translation, code generation, and custom AI commands.[1][2][4] |
| **Customization** | Very limited UI and behavior customization.[1] | Highly customizable: **aliases, hotkeys, themes, custom extensions, layouts, actions**.[1][2][4] |
| **Learning curve** | Almost none; simple and always on.[1] | Moderate: requires initial setup and learning extensions, but then very powerful.[1][2] |
| **Target user** | **Casual users** who just need quick search for apps/files and basic info.[1] | **Power users, developers, and productivity‑focused users** wanting a command center for their Mac.[1][4] |

### Practical takeaway

- Choose **Spotlight** if you want:  
  - Zero setup, built‑in, **simple** search for apps, documents, and basic info.  
  - No desire to customize or pay for extra features.

- Choose **Raycast** if you want:  
  - A **Spotlight replacement** that also acts as clipboard manager, text expander, window manager, command palette, and integration hub for developer and productivity tools.[1][2][4]  
  - Optional **AI‑powered features and cloud sync** via subscription, while keeping most day‑to‑day utilities free.
